activities.name: Happy New Year with Mintu and Herve
activities.description: Bridle Trails was one big muddy puddle. Fun!

activities.name: Small effort is better than no effort
Less than I planned, but more than I enjoyed
activities.description: Nice to be with Mintu and Herve though.

activities.name: Scoping Saturday’s muddy loop, with Mark
activities.description:
Mud, mud, glorious mud.
Nothing quite like it for cooling the blood.
So follow me, follow,
Down to the hollow,
And there we will wallow,
In glorious mud.
(Flanders and Swan)

activities.name: Not a well-shaped run!

activities.name: When the Fremont Bridge is broken it’s hard to run around Lake Union.
activities.description: With Herve, Mintu, and Anita! And now I remember why I stopped wearing my heart rate monitor. It’s giving crazy readings.

Run commute most of the way home

activities.name: Polished Arrowhead

activities.name: La Majahua at sunrise
activities.description: Getting out of the resort bubble onto the dusty streets. Dogs, roosters, and pigs all happy with the new day.

activities.name: Barefoot beach intervals

activities.name: Leg stretcher........ and bonk.
activities.description: That’s what happens when you have half a pastry for lunch.

activities.name: Trying to match Mark D’s pace
activities.description: And not succeeding! He did the loop in 38 mins... I’m  90 seconds behind + I stopped a few times to catch my breath. But not bad for the old man!

activities.name: Seeking sunshine
activities.description: And hills apparently.

activities.name: Distance Ladder Streak, Day 1
activities.description: How many days in a row can I run a little further than I ran the day before? We shall see! I’m starting very low so I have at least one easy week.

activities.name: As the Ox Plows
activities.description: Pushed it a little. Shouldn’t have. Will go slower tomorrow.

activities.name: Reasonably Rectangular Ridge Run
activities.description: Stopped a few times as usual on this one.

activities.name: Approaching octagonal
activities.description: Well... not approaching it very closely.

activities.name: 1 mile on a twisted ankle for Jim Pearson
activities.description: ... whose  streak of running at least 1 mile per day recently passed 50 years. A mildly sprained ankle wouldn’t stop Jim!

activities.name: Sphinx in profile
activities.description: With a little bobbed tail.

activities.name: Read an article about the benefits of shuffling, which actually made me run “Easy”.
activities.description: Worth a read: https://trailrunnermag.com/training/the-art-of-the-easy-shuffle.html

activities.name: Slumped square?
activities.description: Went at it a little too hard. Had to stop a few times.

activities.name: Easy does it on the easy hills
activities.description: Not the most beautiful shaped run. Tomorrow’s will be better!

activities.name: David does a census
activities.description: Every block on (part of) the west facing slope of Tangletown

activities.name: Down to Golden Gardens with Mintu and Anita
activities.description: Keeping our distances on the quiet streets of Sunset Hill and Ballard. Coffee at a distance on our deck to follow

activities.name: Tangletown census tract #2
activities.description: Short and easy, but not easy finding the best path on an non-Eulerian graph. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eulerian_path?wprov=sfti1

activities.name: The horror of sunshine
activities.description: 52F and sunny makes me hot even in a T-shirt. It’s stupid, but that’s the way my thermostat runs

activities.name: Down to the boat house, back up the ravine.
activities.description: I dragged behind Mintu and Anita. Tried to pretend that it was just wise social distancing, but they were not fooled.

activities.name: The heart of Tangletown: graduate level census running
Must have trees!
activities.description: Interlaken is open and beautiful. Prettiest part of the run with Mintu and Anita.

activities.name: Back to the trees with the Tree Lady
And home
activities.description: Didn’t want to spoil the pretty picture of my preceding run.

activities.name: Umm. Let’s not name this shape
Stemless wine glass fell off the table
Tangletown calibration run
activities.description: 43rd run on this route in the last 8 years. Stopped a few times due to sunshine, so true speed slower. Goal is to get back under 9 min pace without stopping.

activities.name: When the streets don’t go through, you get creative
activities.description: With Mintu. The route planning was half the fun.

activities.name: Calibration run #44. Sunny and no stops
Chris and I had quite a knight

activities.name: Telling Lake Union about Green Lake

activities.name: There’s no green like spring green.
activities.description: With Peter who is always up for an adventure.

activities.name: Lake? What lake?
activities.description: Tight chest. Felt a bit similar to when I probably had you know what in March. Hmmm.

activities.name: Tangletown boogie woogie
activities.description: Still a bit constricted.

activities.name: Forest bathing in Interlaken and Arboretum.
Every tangle tells a story
activities.description: This one was sunny, so I had to stop in the shade a few times.

activities.name: When time is short, a little trail is better than no trail.

And a couple of miles more with Mintu
Following Chris on her route through Woodland Park

activities.name: Simplify. Simplify
activities.description: Felt pretty good. Elapsed pace 9:10. Let’s see if I can get it below 9.

activities.name: Simple goal acheived
activities.description: Yesterday’s goal of those (gentle) hills at less than 9 min/mile elapsed time was achieved. Hooray. Next goal: same pace, same terrain for more than 4 miles.

activities.name: Bit of a struggle
activities.description: Some runs comes easily. Others...

activities.name: Simple goal #2 achieved
activities.description: Best short run of the year perhaps. 0 stops. Cool and cloudy just like I like it.

activities.name: Too soon after lunch
activities.description: as my stomach reminded me.

activities.name: 5 before it gets too warm
activities.description: Running before breakfast isn’t my ideal thing, but on a sunny early summer day it’s what I need to do.

activities.name: Up Mt Washington; down Chance Creek
activities.description: Excellent hike with my favorite adventure mate.

activities.name: Checking in with the legs after yesterday's adventure in the mountains
activities.description: Seems OK. Not attempting to run any of it made all the difference.

activities.name: Hilly and chilly in the rain
activities.description: Mintu and Anita are always game.

activities.name: Seeking shade
activities.description: And stopping in it!

activities.name: A little too warm for any further
activities.description: 67 and cloudy. What a heatwave!

activities.name: Back to Bridle Trails
activities.description: Lungs weren’t cooperating. Felt short of breath most of the way. Lots of stops

activities.name: Warm, but no pauses.
activities.description: Did well here.

activities.name: Back to the beautiful Cedar River Trail with Mintu

activities.name: Slow starts suppress subsequent shade stops?

activities.name: Don’t feed the wild ponies
activities.description: Those are the rules of Assateague island. (Hoping to get pony photos soon!)

activities.name: Down Ravenna Canyon and up 22nd Ave.

activities.name: Electric Guitar run with Mintu and Anita
activities.description: Middle six miles had lots of stops and starts. Very fun.

activities.name: Taking Chris to a new trail
70th and the canyon
activities.description: Goal: do this run in under 9:00 mins/mile elapsed pace. This one was a bit over 10 mins/mile

activities.name: Goal achieved!
activities.description: Yesterday I set a goal to shave a minute/mile off my elapsed time for this hilly run. And today I achieved it. Rather surprising, but I’ll take it.

activities.name: July is “Mile Month”
activities.description:
Ok friends... here’s what we are going to do. Everyone will run a measured mile on the track at the end of the month, and will do whatever track work you can before then to get ready for it. Who is in?

Today was my baseline. Tried to run at the 7:00 to 7:15 pace and couldn’t comfortably do more than 1/2 a mile at that pace. Let’s hope things improve from there. I plan to do 1 track workout/week.

activities.name: Chasing Mintu
activities.description: And occasionally begging him to stop

activities.name: Felt pretty good after a day off
activities.description: 8:41 elapsed pace. Next goal is to get a 4 mile version of this run under 8:30

activities.name: Training for the “July Mile”
activities.description: Was able to keep up a 7:30 pace for 3/4 of a mile this time. 7:00 for a mile by the end of the month looks out of reach. 7:15-20 would be a good goal.

activities.name: East Tiger zigzags with Mintu
activities.description: We didn’t visit the East Summit as we usually do, so this was the easy version of this run. Still felt pretty tough!

activities.name: Grunt
activities.description: Aka: hanging clothes on a sloping clothes line

activities.name: When I’m 80, this will be a good run
Reference mile, before next week’s attempt
activities.description: 7:40 for the mile. Hoping to break 7:30 next week on a better track

activities.name: Part 1 of Under and Over Snoqualmie Pass
activities.description: With Mintu and Anita. My watch decided to end the session at the lake. See part 2 for the return

activities.name: Part 2 of Under and Over Snoqualmie Pass: Twin Lakes to Hyak
Slow isn’t always easy
activities.description: Sun was very warm even at 7:00am

activities.name: Not running a mile
activities.description: Did a few half-miles at 7:30, but wasn’t able to keep it going. I have until a Friday for my final July Mile attempt

activities.name: Warm weather slog with Mintu
activities.description: It’s like that in July.

activities.description: This was a hike... took it very easy.

activities.name: Gotta be the most beautiful lake trail around

activities.name: GPS no worky.

activities.name: 10 with Mintu; 5 of then with Anita

activities.name: First run after a week off...
activities.description: ... due to some kind of calf injury. Very sluggish but that’s where it starts

activities.description: Two swims, no summits, but who is counting?

activities.name: The Good Rain
activities.description: Smoke kept me from running for almost two weeks. Might be my longest break in almost 10 years. Starting back nice and easy

activities.name: A lungful of Alpine Lakes and Ridges
activities.description: Anita, Mark, and I went in at the Laura Lake Backdoor on a foggy fall morning, and climbed to the Rampart Lakes and Alta Mountain. Glorious day.

activities.name: The secret of running while aging
activities.description: is the be able to forget how well you were running before your latest setback. And repeat.

activities.name: Little lack of lunch

activities.name: Walk to check accuracy of Garmin “smart mode”
activities.description: Yay. Good enough for what might be 12 hours in the Enchantments Friday!

activities.name: True pace in the hazy fall sunshine
activities.description: Maybe 20 walk breaks

activities.name: Up to Asgard Pass
activities.description: With Steve, Anita, and Mintu

activities.name: Can there be too many larches?
activities.description: This is the season for the Enchantments. Through-hiked it Friday with Steve, Mintu and Anita. Watch disengaged near the top of Asgard Pass for some reason. This is the 2nd part.

activities.name: Running with my sweetie, after a wonderful anniversary dinner
Getting better
activities.description: Best short run in a month or two. Took some sun stops, but hope to do this under 9 pace without them by the end of Oct.

activities.name: Walking the hills on a foggy morning
Smooth and easy til it wasn’t
activities.description: When will I learn to eat lunch properly?

activities.name: Longest run in a while

activities.name: Exploring the neighborhood
activities.description: Jog/walk

activities.name: South Loop of Deception Pass
activities.description: Enjoying the memories of 2017 Deception Pass 25K, which was definitely type 2 fun! #gradientmap

activities.name: Strolling through the big trees with Chris
activities.description: Amazing old growth cedars and Douglas Firs on the Discovery Trail at Deception Pass

activities.name: I’ll meet you on the other side
activities.description: Cascade Lake. I ran one way; Chris, Grace, and Gilad walked the other way.

activities.name: Return to Mountain Lake
activities.description: So fun to show Grace and Gilad my favorite trail on a foggy day

activities.name: Off the clock
activities.description: Watch wasn't changed and wanted the experience of NOT worrying about pace.

activities.name: Discovering Dayton
activities.description: Linden to 79th to Dayton to the zoo is the longest and hence easiest way to climb Phinney. If I can improve my lungs I should be able to do this loop under the 9min pace.

activities.name: Gedye family on the Blaine St stairs
activities.description: Chris did 3x; David 4x; and Grace 5x. A nice Pythagorean triple!

activities.name: Autumn leaves of Woodland Park and Phinney, with Grace!
Haven’t run at work for almost 8 months
Sergiy’s loop with Anita and Mintu
activities.description:
Glorious frosty fall morning. What beautiful trails. Thank you Sergiy!
This GPS was a bit generous. My watch said 9.0 miles.

activities.name: Pace ladder on my home hill
activities.description: It’s easier if you start really slow!

activities.name: Up 50th and down Dayton
activities.description: Went a little too fast up... and had to walk on the way down. Next time I aim to balance it better and average under 9.

activities.name: New shoes on a sparkling fall day

activities.description: With Mintu and Anita of course!

activities.name: Trees of Green Lake
activities.description: Chris and I actually walked around Green Lake together!

activities.name: Do flu shots help your running?
activities.description: Mine seemed to today!

activities.name: Short run before the election results
Bonk happens
activities.description: Did 2 of 5 miles nicely then fell off a cliff. I’ll take it.

activities.name: Hooray! “Down Dayton” loop under 9
activities.description: In blustery conditions no less.

activities.name: President Biden!
activities.description: With Anita and Mintu

activities.name: Runs better when cold
activities.description: Mid 30s are my perfect running temps

activities.name: Trying not to push it
activities.description: ... and I go almost the same speed as when I am pushing it. There’s a lesson here.

activities.name: Pushed fairly hard
activities.description: And made up a minute. 3rd good run in 3 days. I’m a happy chap.

activities.name: Suddenly sunny

activities.name: Back to the Cedar River Trail

activities.name: Little recovery after yesterday’s exertions
activities.description: Felt as ungainly as this path looks.

activities.name: Be-drenched, bedraggled, and bespattered
activities.description: Exquisitely bad timing in the downpours. The joys of running in November in Seattle.

activities.name: I don’t mind rainy and windy
activities.description: But I draw the line at rainy, windy, and dark.

activities.name: Smoothly up Dayton
activities.description: And achieved my goal of a 9 min pace. Hooray.

activities.name: A spear jabbing Lake City

activities.name: Mud puddles of Bridle Trails

activities.name: Rolling Hills of Loyal Heights

activities.name: Checking out the old growth of Seward Park

activities.name: Solid pace for tired legs

activities.name: Bluebird day on Phinney Ridge

activities.name: Strange Jordan Curve
activities.description: Going a little further south and north than usual to prevent self-crossings. Stopped a few times.

activities.name: Easy then moderate then...

activities.name: Testing  the birthday present from my running mates
activities.description: In the dark of Woodland Park it works great. Thank you Herve, Ana, Mintu, and Anita. Next stop: self organized small group night run in Bridle Trails. You have been warned!

activities.name: PR on my favorite home route

activities.name: ‘Tis the season

activities.name: Tricked by 10 minutes of sunshine between the downpours
